reachability_artifacts: built sworn replan migrate/activate against an isolated committed copy of the seven-slice local-first plan plus equivalent MCP calls, proving all 17 mutations including contracts consumers, receipt identities, restart propagation, retirement of four direct MCP planning writers, all-or-nothing failure, and zero mutation of the live downstream release

open_deferrals.item: Apply the proven v0.15 migration to the live 2026-07-15-local-first-account-safety release.
open_deferrals.why: The live migration must run only after this conformance release is integrated and the new engine is the current protocol authority; applying it inside the engine's own delivery track would use unintegrated authority and blur Planner role separation.
open_deferrals.acknowledgement: The Coach ratified that the pristine local-first plan is the first required downstream migration after the conformance release is integrated.

release_benefit: Human-led re-planning can be applied and recovered mechanically without corrupting lifecycle authority or fabricating migration evidence.

design_decisions.choice: Keep re-plan meaning human-led while one engine operation applies only a ratified delta, and permit in-place migration only for pristine planned records.
options: human judgment plus deterministic engine application with pristine-only migration
role sessions directly mutate authority records
binary invents decomposition and migrates any unshipped state
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified by Brad (Coach), 2026-07-15: Planner owns meaning, the engine owns exact application, and only pristine planned records may migrate in place.
design_decisions.rationale: The boundary preserves qualitative human judgment while preventing retroactive fabrication of lifecycle evidence.

design_decisions.choice: Use a canonical non-self-referential Planner ref, byte-pinned plan deltas, a deterministic receipt, Git-derived propagation state, and a separate migrated-only activation edge.
options: canonical Planner ref plus byte-pinned delta, Git-derived topology, and separate migrated activation
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified under Brad's 2026-07-16 instruction to proceed with the orchestrator recommendation: adopt the referenced v1 schemas, canonical Planner ref, deterministic receipt, compare-and-swap propagation topology, and distinct migrated-release activation.
design_decisions.rationale: Separating source identity from the commit containing the delta removes self-reference; keeping transaction and activation state derivable from Git makes crash recovery exact without replaying Planner judgment or misusing native C-13 authority.

positive_scenarios: A ratified delta is seeded from the exact owner track and a fully pristine seven-slice plan migrates all 17 blobs, including contracts consumers, atomically with before/after receipts.
negative_scenarios: A started record, stale source/contract identity, direct legacy MCP planning write, lifecycle mutation beyond the ratified rollback link, or propagation divergence blocks without partial writes or force.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Separating human planning judgment from deterministic application makes re-plan and migration safe for autonomous orchestration.
